task_completion.description: Microsoft Edge is used to access the target web applications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Microsoft Edge browser window is visible, navigating to at least one of the target URLs (photopea.com, excalidraw.com, or devdocs.io).

task_completion.description: Photopea is installed as a Progressive Web App.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Photopea is seen running in a standalone Edge app window (lacking the standard browser address bar and tabs), OR the Edge 'Install app' dialog for Photopea is visible.

task_completion.description: Excalidraw is installed as a Progressive Web App.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Excalidraw is seen running in a standalone Edge app window (lacking the standard browser address bar and tabs), OR the Edge 'Install app' dialog for Excalidraw is visible.

task_completion.description: DevDocs is installed as a Progressive Web App.
task_completion.visual_evidence: DevDocs is seen running in a standalone Edge app window (lacking the standard browser address bar and tabs), OR the Edge 'Install app' dialog for DevDocs is visible.

task_completion.description: The installed PWAs are accessible as standalone apps in the system menu.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OS system application menu/launcher is open and displays the icons for Photopea, Excalidraw, and DevDocs, OR a file manager/terminal shows their newly created .desktop files in /home/ga/.local/share/applications.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Microsoft Edge browser GUI to install the apps rather than manually spoofing .desktop files via terminal or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Microsoft Edge GUI being interacted with (e.g., clicking the 'App available' icon in the address bar or using the Edge menu to install).

integrity.description: The applications are installed as true PWAs, not just bookmarked or opened in regular browser tabs.
integrity.visual_evidence: The apps should appear in standalone windows without standard browser navigation controls (URL bar, tabs), or the system menu should show them as distinct applications.

integrity.description: The apps are named exactly as requested without extra text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The names in the system menu or app window title bars should be exactly 'Photopea', 'Excalidraw', and 'DevDocs'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target applications are Photopea (photopea.com), Excalidraw (excalidraw.com), and DevDocs (devdocs.io). All three are legitimate Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) that can be installed via Microsoft Edge. When installed as PWAs on Linux via Edge, they create .desktop files in the standard XDG directory: /home/ga/.local/share/applications. The VLM should look for these apps running in standalone windows (without standard browser address bars) or their presence in the system application launcher.
